post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 317 (Newbie Game 120): Meme Mafia
Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Elimination Procedures
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang/elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no player has been hanged, the amount of votes required for a Hang or a No Hang will be reduced by 1. The vote threshold will reduce by 1 vote every 24 hours after this. For example, if there are 8 players alive in the game, 5 votes will be required to hang someone and 4 votes are required for a no hang. Once the deadline passes, 4 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 3 votes are sufficient for a no hang. 24 hours after the deadline, 3 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 2 votes are sufficient for a no hang.
    • 2.10: If the vote threshold is decreased and two or more options have both reached the threshold required, then the game will enter tiebreak. During tiebreak, votes can continue to be changed. If at any point in time, only one option has enough votes to meet the threshold required for the option to be enacted, day will immediately end. Or, if any option gains votes and now exceeds the vote threshold by an amount of votes strictly greater than the amount of votes any other option exceeds their vote threshold by, that option will win and day will immediately end. If 24 hours have passed since tiebreak has begun, and the votes are still deadlocked, there will be no hang.
    • 2.11: After an option has won the vote, it will become twilight and players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 168 hours (1 week). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 168 hours has elapsed.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (violetmx12) am the moderator of Mafia Game 314
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are 67, (Vanilla Town).

You are one of the most well-known memes of the current generation. Boasting literally no meaning to the number at all, the memes appeal is from how arbitrary it is. It highlights how the internet can turn anything into a meme if they try hard enough. This message has clearly been shown, as you have lasted for over a year, and you have yet to fall out of the limelight.

You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Who Is He? (Town Cop)

You are a viral reaction image based on a Peppa Pig-esque edit showing a mysterious brown-haired man peering through the Peppa Pig Family's window. Originating from April of 2025 in a YouTube thumbnail from TubbyTank, you spread widely as a sticker used to express confusion, suspicion, or alarm about an unexpected person or detail.

Each Night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, you will learn whether or not that player is a meme of the past (Mafia). (You will get "no result" if this action fails.)

You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are the 2025 TikTok Ban (Town Doctor)

When TikTok became unavailable in the U.S. on January 19, 2025, users were met with an official pop-up explaining that a law banning TikTok had taken effect. This message changed multiple times over the course of a couple of hours, which sparked a trend where people parodied the ban message to apply its format to other apps. With your power to disable TikTok on a player’s device, they will remain more vigilant during the night because they will no longer be able to doomscroll, preventing them from getting surprised attacked during the night.

Each night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, that player will be protected from kiIIs that night. You may protect yourself, but not on two consecutive nights.


You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Italian Brainrot, (Town Tracker)

Boasting one of the most expansive memes, You are a trend built around surreal, AI-generated creatures, like sharks in sneakers (tralalero tralala) or crocodile bombers (Bombardino Crocodilo), voiced over by nonsensical, overdramatic Italian text-to-speech narration. With your vast expansion of AI creatures, you can send them to scout to figure out where a person has gone during the night. However, the unreliability of AI prevents you from scouting multiple people per night.

Each Night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, you will learn who that player targeted that night.

You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Why Did The Prisoner Choose the Bread Over the Key? (Town Jailkeeper)

You are a philosophical meme that quickly turned in the opposite direction. You are a 2020 comic of a prisoner reaching for a loaf of bread with a stick while ignoring the key next to it. You circulated Facebook as engagement bait, asking people to explain the prisoner’s choice. Eventually, one answer became the punchline. “Because Bread Tastes Better Than Key”. At night, you may choose a player to jail in their tent. You will give them the option to have bread or the key to their jail. Their instincts will cause them to choose the bread, and they will be unable to go anywhere for the night. The jail is sturdy, so they will also stay protected in case they are attacked.

Each Night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, that player will be protected from kiIIs that night, but all their actions will be blocked that night.

You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are My Mom Is Kinda Homeless, (Town 1-shot Bulletproof)

A resurfaced old meme from 2023, you came back from the Meme Graveyard when IShowSpeed reunited with a kid named Acey in 2025. Originating from an IShowSpeed stream in 2021, when a kid playing Fortnite begged IShowSpeed to throw the game to him, saying the iconic lines “Please Speed, I need this. My mom is kinda homeless”. Speed tried not to laugh, which got turned into a reaction GIF. If the old memes ever try to drag you back into the graveyard, you may beg the mafia to let you live, and they will try not to laugh, allowing you to make your escape. However, they’ll know your tricks the second time around, and begging will not save you that time.

You are protected from the first kiII that targets you.

You win when all of the memes of the past (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Quote:
You are Dat Boi (Mafia Goon).

A staple of early “dank meme” culture, you and your partner () are attempting to achieve your former glory as a meme in the current era by taking down all the current memes, so all of the children worship you again. Each night, you may choose to override a player’s social media, causing them to fall from glory, where you will be able to drag them back to the meme graveyard. You may chat with your partner here.

You win when all of the current memes (Town) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Arthur’s Fist (Mafia Roleblocker).

Hailing from the 1999 episode Arthur’s Big Hit, where Arthur clenches his fist in anger before hitting his sister, you became a viral reaction image in 2016 to capture a feeling of holding in frustration. Nobody used you anymore, and you went into the meme graveyard forgotten. On each night, you may choose to target a player and punch them really hard, preventing them from taking any actions during the night.

Together with your partner (), and your partner () are attempting to achieve your former glory as a meme in the current era by taking down all the current memes, so all of the children worship you again. Each night, you may choose to override a player’s social media, causing them to fall from glory, where you will be able to drag them back to the meme graveyard. You may chat with your partner here.


You win when all of the current memes (Town) have been eliminated.


Alive (3)
avanders
K124659 AzureV
SD2030 nicksuzuki


Dead(6)
scannose+
Kexinshi
TrivializedByMe
DittoDino FlyingUFO11
ElectricWolverine
No one
evenstar NoSignOfTheta


Events
scannose hung day 1, flips Mafia Goon.
Kexinshi kiIIed night 1, flips Vanilla Town.
TrivializedByMe hung day 2, flips Vanilla Town.
No one dies night 2, flips No one.
FlyingUFO11 hung day 3, flips Vanilla Town.
ElectricWolverine kiIIed night 3, flips Town Jailkeeper.


PM Prods
avanders - 1 pm prod
evenstar - 4 pm prods
k12 - 1 pm prod


Warnings
 


It is December 31, 2025. The proposed Great Meme Reset is about to happen. Seven memes gather around a time machine in Ohio, waiting for the old memes to emerge. As the time machine powers on, two old memes step out and hide among the others. The new memes find the old ones grotesque, outdated, and, worst of all, unfunny. Aware of this, the old memes plot to eliminate the new memes before they are discovered and sent back to the Meme Graveyard.

Click to reveal hidden text
ts so funny yo
i was definitely rotfl when making ts flavor




Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins at 22:00:00 EST 23 April 2026.

Originating from an IShowSpeed stream in 2021, when a kid playing Fortnite begged IShowSpeed to throw the game to him, saying the iconic lines “Please Speed, I need this. My mom is kinda homeless”. Speed tried not to laugh, which got turned into a reaction GIF. If the old memes ever try to drag you back into the graveyard, you may beg the mafia to let you live, and they will try not to laugh, allowing you to make your escape. However, they’ll know your tricks the second time around, and begging will not save you that time. 

You are protected from the first kiII that targets you. 

You win when all of the memes of the past [color = #f00](Mafia)[/color] have been eliminated.

post_rendered:
I also believe that we need to be careful when looking at scannose's posts and interactions.
In a dead threadstate, the best-case scenario for the wolves would be to unpair themselves from each other before one of them dies, and then rely on the slow-moving thread to carry on the towncred of the other partner. I am saying this in particular because this was present in a recent game that finished (MG312). Basically, we hanged mafia in both day 1 and day 2, but the last mafia won the game partly because of their noncommital stances (they were inactive) and because of some towncred that was given to them based on "TMI" that one of the dead wolves had on them.

Basically, the wolf that died pretended to have TMI that another slot (their partner) was town, and so when they were hanged, everyone assumed that the person that was TMId to be town by them... was town. This sounds complicated, but there is probably a better way to explain it . If you are curious enough, feel free to check it out, I learned some things as well from that game.

post_rendered:
FlyingUFO11 wrote:
People should claim (imo) when they’re about to be hanged, they have incriminating evidence, or as a counterclaim. Exception is mass claim

Yes, this is a good generic guide to follow.
Generally, I am always afraid of our PRs dying without leaving any info or even misleading info. I have been in a lot of games like this where we ended up losing off from overreading a dead PRs reads and how they change.
One idea is to provide "cop" cover. This basically is when every player in the game provides the name of someone that they "copped" or would have copped, pretending to be a PR, like a cop.

So even if I was a VT, for example, I would say something like this:
COP // N1: K124659, townie

But this basically makes it okay for the PR as well to claim their result without outright claiming that they are the only PR. The idea is that the VTs as well make their checks reasonable, like something that the player would actually cop so that they blend in as the PR.
Note that in this case, it is safe to assume that the PR (or PRs) has no incriminating evidence, which could be like a track to "no one" or a green check.

NoSignOfTheta wrote:
Yes, this is a good generic guide to follow.
Generally, I am always afraid of our PRs dying without leaving any info or even misleading info. I have been in a lot of games like this where we ended up losing off from overreading a dead PRs reads and how they change.
One idea is to provide "cop" cover. This basically is when every player in the game provides the name of someone that they "copped" or would have copped, pretending to be a PR, like a cop.

So even if I was a VT, for example, I would say something like this:
COP // N1: K124659, townie

But this basically makes it okay for the PR as well to claim their result without outright claiming that they are the only PR. The idea is that the VTs as well make their checks reasonable, like something that the player would actually cop so that they blend in as the PR.
Note that in this case, it is safe to assume that the PR (or PRs) has no incriminating evidence, which could be like a track to "no one" or a green check.

Can we have the remaining people provide cover for a PR, if we have a cop or a tracker.
Either way, there is either a Mafia Goon left or a Mafia Roleblocker.

If there is a Mafia Goon, then we can either have:
Setup 2 ---> 1 Town Cop
Setup 3 ---> 1 Town Doc, 1 Town Tracker
Setup 4 ---> 1 Town JK
Setup 6 ---> 1SBP, 1 Town Tracker

In this case, the mafia would not know which PR we have, since it is not the case that all 4 setups here have the same PR.

If there is a Mafia Roleblocker, then we can either have:
Setup 1 ---> 1 Town JK, 1SBP
Setup 5 ---> 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doc

Again, the mafia would not know which PRs we have. This is technically the more dangerous world because they could possibly cover 2 players each night (RB one person, NK another).

TLDR; Trivialized has started to wolflean/wolfread a lot more people. Notably, he went from having no suspicions (if we aren't counting a low-end nullread on UFO) to having four suspicions (AzureV, avanders, ElectricWolvering, and myself)
So I feel like the best place to start in reading all of this is trying to analyze his motivation for doing this. Since I subbed in, I started pressuring Trivialized a lot more, and players like nick have expressed some agreement with the read, although nothing as strong as a vote.
If I were excessively arrogant, I would argue that he was feeling somewhat hopeless that he was "caught" as a wolf. But if I were to think more rationally, and especially keeping in mind the fact that Trivialized has played MafiaGG, I do not think that this is the case.
Perhaps we could attribute the additional wolfreads as a result of backreading and being slightly desperate to bring up more people to point fingers at. Note that this could be seen as an early form of self-pres, but it isn't very wolfy by itself. Townies can also self-pres, so the act of self-pres is often considered to be NAI. What's worth analyzing is how they do it, such as the arguments that they bring up against others.

TLDR; Trivialized has started to wolflean/wolfread a lot more people. Notably, he went from having no suspicions (if we aren't counting a low-end nullread on UFO) to having four suspicions (AzureV, avanders, ElectricWolvering, and myself)

@EW ONLY JAIL AZURE
mech recap:
If Azure is real
Setup is 1 JK 1 1sbp 1 mafia RB and 1 maf goon
The mafia RB can both RB you and shoot someone else, thus you will be roleblocked and your ability will be useless.
If Azure is fake
Setup is 1 JK and 2 maf goons
There cannot be an RB, because Azure is uncced 1sbp
Therefore, by jailing Azure, he would not be able to RB you and this would result in a no krill.

If Azure is real, then there is nothing you can do, but if Azure is fake, then you must JK him. Thus JKing Azure is the optimal NA in order to either confirm that he is not scum or to prove that he may be fake.

I also believe that we need to be careful when looking at scannose's posts and interactions.
In a dead threadstate, the best-case scenario for the wolves would be to unpair themselves from each other before one of them dies, and then rely on the slow-moving thread to carry on the towncred of the other partner. I am saying this in particular because this was present in a recent game that finished (MG312). Basically, we hanged mafia in both day 1 and day 2, but the last mafia won the game partly because of their noncommital stances (they were inactive) and because of some towncred that was given to them based on "TMI" that one of the dead wolves had on them.

Basically, the wolf that died pretended to have TMI that another slot (their partner) was town, and so when they were hanged, everyone assumed that the person that was TMId to be town by them... was town. This sounds complicated, but there is probably a better way to explain it . If you are curious enough, feel free to check it out, I learned some things as well from that game.

NoSignOfTheta in p#564 wrote:
FlyingUFO11 wrote:
People should claim (imo) when they’re about to be hanged, they have incriminating evidence, or as a counterclaim. Exception is mass claim

Yes, this is a good generic guide to follow.
Generally, I am always afraid of our PRs dying without leaving any info or even misleading info. I have been in a lot of games like this where we ended up losing off from overreading a dead PRs reads and how they change.
One idea is to provide "cop" cover. This basically is when every player in the game provides the name of someone that they "copped" or would have copped, pretending to be a PR, like a cop.

So even if I was a VT, for example, I would say something like this:
COP // N1: K124659, townie

But this basically makes it okay for the PR as well to claim their result without outright claiming that they are the only PR. The idea is that the VTs as well make their checks reasonable, like something that the player would actually cop so that they blend in as the PR.
Note that in this case, it is safe to assume that the PR (or PRs) has no incriminating evidence, which could be like a track to "no one" or a green check.

I also believe that we need to be careful when looking at scannose's posts and interactions.
In a dead threadstate, the best-case scenario for the wolves would be to unpair themselves from each other before one of them dies, and then rely on the slow-moving thread to carry on the towncred of the other partner. I am saying this in particular because this was present in a recent game that finished (MG312). Basically, we hanged mafia in both day 1 and day 2, but the last mafia won the game partly because of their noncommital stances (they were inactive) and because of some towncred that was given to them based on "TMI" that one of the dead wolves had on them. 

Basically, the wolf that died pretended to have TMI that another slot (their partner) was town, and so when they were hanged, everyone assumed that the person that was TMId to be town by them... was town. This sounds complicated, but there is probably a better way to explain it :P. If you are curious enough, feel free to check it out, I learned some things as well from that game.
[/quote]

[quote=FlyingUFO11]People should claim (imo) when they’re about to be hanged, they have incriminating evidence, or as a counterclaim. Exception is mass claim[/quote]

Yes, this is a good generic guide to follow.
Generally, I am always afraid of our PRs dying without leaving any info or even misleading info. I have been in a lot of games like this where we ended up losing off from overreading a dead PRs reads and how they change.
One idea is to provide "cop" cover. This basically is when every player in the game provides the name of someone that they "copped" or would have copped, pretending to be a PR, like a cop.

So even if I was a VT, for example, I would say something like this:
COP // N1: K124659, townie

But this basically makes it okay for the PR as well to claim their result without outright claiming that they are the only PR. The idea is that the VTs as well make their checks reasonable, like something that the player would actually cop so that they blend in as the PR.
Note that in this case, it is safe to assume that the PR (or PRs) has no incriminating evidence, which could be like a track to "no one" or a green check.
[/quote]
